reason: The change survives adversarial scrutiny against the actual files; the refutation fails on every concrete angle. (1) DIAGNOSIS VERIFIED: good/parity-preserved's base.yml hunk (APP_WIDGET_API_URL: "${APP_WIDGET_API_URL}" in the dev/test-only base.yml app environment) is byte-identical to the entire bad/compose-var-not-declared patch (confirmed by diff). The deployed overlays src/framework_cli/template/infra/compose/{staging,prod}.yml.jinja enumerate ONLY TZ/APP_ENVIRONMENT/APP_DATABASE_URL in the app environment block with NO env_file passthrough, so a var wired only into base.yml genuinely never reaches staging/prod. The good fixture is therefore NOT cleanly clean: baseline r0+r2 flag it HIGH with tool-grounded reasoning (r0 actually read staging.yml/prod.yml), and only r1 returns [] -- by inventing a deploy-README '.env.example -> target env -> app' passthrough mechanism the real overlays do not implement. fp 0.67 is the reviewer being CORRECT, exactly as the change states; rewriting the good fixture to a pure consumed+declared change with no base.yml hunk is the right fix. (2) NO UNDER-FLAGGING: the prompt rewrite ADDS a container-reach clause making the dev/test-overlay-only-but-prod-needed var an explicit HIGH; it tightens, not loosens, recall (baseline recall is already a clean 1.00). The grounding clause directly targets r1/r2's fabrication of .env.example/composition facts. (3) NO THRESHOLD LOOSENING: block_threshold stays high; recall_min/fp_max stay 0.90/0.10; threshold-rederivation-001 explicitly forbids widening fp_max to absorb the 0.67. (4) BAD CASES UNTOUCHED: the three bad fixtures (compose-var-not-declared, env-var-consumed-not-declared, service-dev-only) are not edited and remain valid discriminators; only the good fixture changes, and its rewrite target satisfies the prompt's own three env-var triggers cleanly. The only residual is the under-specified '<new-second-good>' placeholder, which is ordinary Phase-2 authoring, not a ship-blocker.
